summary: Excellent matrix recording of a jazzy ’75 show featuring rare Blues For Allah, a transcendent Peggy‑O and a smoking Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence.
review: The matrix blend balances the booming audience tape with the flatter soundboard feed to give a crystal‑clear, immersive listening experience. Musically the night shines with a once‑in‑a‑lifetime Blues For Allah, an unforgettable Peggy‑O, King Solomon’s Marbles and a superb Help > Slip > Franklin’s run, plus Garcia’s monster Crazy Fingers and stellar keyboard work. Overall it is hailed as one of 1975’s most underrated concerts.

ai_rating.rationale: All six detailed reviews give five‑star ratings, praising both the performance and the matrix sound quality; the show is described as exceptional with multiple standout jams, while the recording is called superb and well balanced.

recording_quality.technical_notes: The matrix expertly blends the audience ambience with the flat SBD feed, resulting in crisp, clear sound with no noticeable drop‑outs or muddiness. Reviewers repeatedly call it “superb” and note that the balancing makes the performance vivid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the concert as jazzy and jam‑heavy, with a fluid second set that moves from Blues For Allah into King Solomon’s Marbles and then launches into an extended Help>Slip>Franklin’s. The rare tunes and seamless transitions give the show a special, almost cinematic flow.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is praised as the centerpiece of the night – his tone on Crazy Fingers and the extended jams is called perfect and “acidic dead”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work on the slower Franklin’s tempo is highlighted as reminiscent of the studio version, showing his melodic approach.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s contributions are acknowledged positively, with a note that few can match Garcia’s nightly lead role.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (keyboardist) receives high marks for his Fender Rhodes work throughout the show.
